problem: In a small town, there are three water tanks: Tank A, Tank B, and Tank C. Tank A can hold 2000 liters, Tank B can hold 3000 liters, and Tank C can hold 4000 liters. The tanks are currently filled to the following levels: Tank A has 1200 liters, Tank B has 1800 liters, and Tank C has 2800 liters. Water is being added to the tanks at the following rates: 10 liters per minute to Tank A, 15 liters per minute to Tank B, and 20 liters per minute to Tank C. However, due to a leak, water is also being lost from the tanks at the following rates: 2 liters per minute from Tank A, 3 liters per minute from Tank B, and 5 liters per minute from Tank C. If the town needs at least 6000 liters of water to meet the daily demand and all three tanks are filled and emptied simultaneously, how many minutes will it take to have at least 6000 liters of water in the tanks, assuming the filling and leaking rates remain constant?
reasoning_steps: Step 1: Calculate the net rate at which water is being added to each tank per minute. For Tank A, this is 10 liters/minute - 2 liters/minute = 8 liters/minute. For Tank B, this is 15 liters/minute - 3 liters/minute = 12 liters/minute. For Tank C, this is 20 liters/minute - 5 liters/minute = 15 liters/minute.
Step 2: Determine the current total amount of water in all three tanks. This is 1200 liters (Tank A) + 1800 liters (Tank B) + 2800 liters (Tank C) = 5800 liters.
Step 3: Calculate the additional amount of water needed to meet the daily demand of at least 6000 liters. This is 6000 liters - 5800 liters = 200 liters.
Step 4: Calculate the combined rate at which water is being added to all three tanks. This is 8 liters/minute (Tank A) + 12 liters/minute (Tank B) + 15 liters/minute (Tank C) = 35 liters/minute.

Step 5: Determine how many minutes it will take to accumulate the additional 200 liters needed. This is 200 liters / 35 liters/minute = 5.71 minutes. Since the tanks cannot be filled for a fraction of a minute in this context, we round up to the nearest whole minute to ensure the demand is met.
